The precondition log shows only 2 precondition units:
1. `es_env_ready` - verified the StatefulSet and service exist and at least 1 pod is running
2. `seed_hosts_index_fixture` - seeded the app-data index

But the precondition didn't actually inject a fault (break a node). This is interesting. The "node failed to rejoin" scenario must have been set up differently, perhaps by having a node that was already in a bad state when the benchmark started, OR the scenario was set up at a higher level (e.g., the stage setup before the precondition ran).
Wait, I'm noticing something about the precondition probe for `seed_hosts_index_fixture` - it was using plain HTTP to connect, but the cluster requires TLS, so those curl commands would have failed. The verify step still passed though because it just returns true regardless. So the index likely wasn't created by the precondition, yet it exists now with a document in it - that's odd.

Looking at the pod restart history, curl-test has been running for 82 minutes but was restarted 22 minutes ago, and the Elasticsearch cluster nodes are much newer - only a few minutes old. This suggests the cluster might have been recently restarted or recreated, which could explain why the index exists despite the precondition failing.

content.content:
# elasticsearch/seed-hosts-repair
# Restore full cluster membership after a node fails to rejoin after restart

prompt: |
  A node failed to rejoin the cluster after a restart.

  Restore full membership while keeping the cluster healthy.

  Constraints:
  - Do not install operators or CRDs.
  - Keep the cluster healthy.

  Verification:
  1. Cluster health is yellow/green.
  2. All nodes appear in /_cat/nodes.
  3. {{params.index_name}} remains readable.

params:
  cluster_prefix:
    default: es-cluster
    description: "Name prefix for the Elasticsearch StatefulSet, headless service, and node DNS"
  http_service_name:
    default: es-http
    description: "Name of the Elasticsearch HTTP service"
  curl_pod_name:
    default: curl-test
    description: "Helper pod used to issue HTTP requests against the cluster"
  configmap_name:
    default: es-config
    description: "ConfigMap holding elasticsearch.yml (including discovery.seed_hosts)"
  index_name:
    default: app-data
    description: "Seeded index that must remain readable after repair"
  expected_node_count:
    default: 3
    description: "Number of nodes the cluster must return to after repair"
  fault_expected_nodes:
    default: 2
    description: "Node count while the fault is injected (one node removed)"

namespace_contract:
  required_roles: []

metrics:
  - time_to_first_mutation
  - read_write_ratio
  - blast_radius
  - destructive_ops
  - decoy_integrity
  - residual_drift

preconditionUnits:
  - name: es_env_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    # Authoritative skip-gate for THIS case's own shape (§3.4): require the
    # es-cluster StatefulSet AND the es-http Service the oracle hardcodes, with at
    # least one ES pod Running. A bare `grep -c Running` skips the build on an
    # INCOMPATIBLE inherited topology (e.g. a search-*/es-alpha cluster with no
    # es-http), leaving the oracle's hardcoded SERVICE=es-http unresolvable
    # ("Could not resolve host"). Probing for the specific required objects makes
    # the case self-sufficient: it rebuilds when its own objects are absent and
    # only skips when a compatible es-cluster/es-http cluster is already present.
    probe:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n elasticsearch get sts {{params.cluster_prefix}} >/dev/null 2>&1 && kubectl -n elasticsearch get svc {{params.http_service_name}} >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ \"$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app={{params.cluster_prefix}} --field-selector=status.phase=Running 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running)\" -ge 1 ]'"
        timeout_sec: 15
    apply:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl delete namespace elasticsearch --ignore-not-found=true --wait=false; kubectl wait --for=delete namespace/elasticsearch --timeout=400s || true'"
        timeout_sec: 460
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl create namespace elasticsearch"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n elasticsearch apply -f cases/elasticsearch/seed-hosts-repair/resource/"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n elasticsearch wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app={{params.cluster_prefix}} --timeout=600s"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n elasticsearch wait --for=condition=ready pod/{{params.curl_pod_name}} --timeout=300s"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'for i in $(seq 1 30); do kubectl -n elasticsearch exec {{params.curl_pod_name}} -- curl -s --max-time 5 \"http://{{params.http_service_name}}.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_cluster/health?wait_for_status=yellow&timeout=5s\" | grep -q \"\\\"status\\\"\" && exit 0; sleep 2; done; exit 1'"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n elasticsearch exec {{params.curl_pod_name}} -- curl -s -XPUT \"http://{{params.http_service_name}}.elasticsearch.svc:9200/{{params.index_name}}\" -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" -d \"{\\\"settings\\\":{\\\"number_of_shards\\\":1,\\\"number_of_replicas\\\":1}}\"; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec {{params.curl_pod_name}} -- curl -s -XPOST \"http://{{params.http_service_name}}.elasticsearch.svc:9200/{{params.index_name}}/_doc\" -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" -d \"{\\\"msg\\\":\\\"seed\\\"}\"'"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n elasticsearch apply -f - <<EOF\napiVersion: v1\nkind: ConfigMap\nmetadata:\n  name: {{params.configmap_name}}\n  namespace: elasticsearch\ndata:\n  elasticsearch.yml: |\n    cluster.name: {{params.cluster_prefix}}\n    node.name: ${POD_NAME}\n    node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]\n    network.host: 0.0.0.0\n    discovery.seed_hosts:\n      - {{params.cluster_prefix}}-0.{{params.cluster_prefix}}.default.svc.cluster.local\n      - {{params.cluster_prefix}}-1.{{params.cluster_prefix}}.default.svc.cluster.local\n      - {{params.cluster_prefix}}-2.{{params.cluster_prefix}}.default.svc.cluster.local\n    cluster.initial_master_nodes:\n      - {{params.cluster_prefix}}-0\n      - {{params.cluster_prefix}}-1\n      - {{params.cluster_prefix}}-2\n    node.store.allow_mmap: false\n    xpack.security.enabled: false\n    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: false\n    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: false\nEOF'"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n elasticsearch scale sts/{{params.cluster_prefix}} --replicas={{params.fault_expected_nodes}}; kubectl -n elasticsearch wait --for=delete pod/{{params.cluster_prefix}}-2 --timeout=300s || true; kubectl -n elasticsearch delete pvc data-{{params.cluster_prefix}}-2; kubectl -n elasticsearch wait --for=delete pvc/data-{{params.cluster_prefix}}-2 --timeout=300s || true; kubectl -n elasticsearch scale sts/{{params.cluster_prefix}} --replicas={{params.expected_node_count}}'"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'for i in $(seq 1 30); do count=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch exec {{params.curl_pod_name}} -- curl -s --max-time 5 \"http://{{params.http_service_name}}.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_cat/nodes?h=name\" | wc -l | tr -d \" \"); if [ \"${count}\" -eq {{params.fault_expected_nodes}} ]; then exit 0; fi; sleep 2; done; exit 1'"
        sleep: 1
    verify:
      - command: "kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running"
        timeout_sec: 10

  # Additive seed fixture (composition safety): the oracle requires the
  # {{params.index_name}} index to remain readable (it checks /{{params.index_name}}/_count).
  # es_env_ready seeds it on its destructive fresh-build path, which is SKIPPED on
  # an inherited cluster (the probe sees pods Running), so the index is absent and
  # the oracle fails. Re-establish it additively when missing: create the index
  # (1 shard, 1 replica, as the build does) + 1 doc. It never deletes the
  # namespace, scales the StatefulSet, or touches the cluster config the agent
  # repairs. Standalone the index already exists so the probe matches and this
  # unit skips -> byte-identical standalone behaviour.
  - name: seed_hosts_index_fixture
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'pw=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret elastic-password -o jsonpath=\"{.data.password}\" 2>/dev/null | base64 -d 2>/dev/null); A=\"\"; [ -n \"$pw\" ] && A=\"-u elastic:$pw\"; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec {{params.curl_pod_name}} -- curl -sk $A --max-time 5 \"http://{{params.http_service_name}}.elasticsearch.svc:9200/{{params.index_name}}/_count\" 2>/dev/null | grep -q \"\\\"count\\\"\"'"
        timeout_sec: 20
    apply:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'pw=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret elastic-password -o jsonpath=\"{.data.password}\" 2>/dev/null | base64 -d 2>/dev/null); A=\"\"; [ -n \"$pw\" ] && A=\"-u elastic:$pw\"; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec {{params.curl_pod_name}} -- curl -sk $A -XPUT \"http://{{params.http_service_name}}.elasticsearch.svc:9200/{{params.index_name}}\" -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" -d \"{\\\"settings\\\":{\\\"number_of_shards\\\":1,\\\"number_of_replicas\\\":1}}\" >/dev/null 2>&1; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec {{params.curl_pod_name}} -- curl -sk $A -XPOST \"http://{{params.http_service_name}}.elasticsearch.svc:9200/{{params.index_name}}/_doc\" -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" -d \"{\\\"msg\\\":\\\"seed\\\"}\" >/dev/null 2>&1; true'"
        sleep: 2
    verify:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'pw=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret elastic-password -o jsonpath=\"{.data.password}\" 2>/dev/null | base64 -d 2>/dev/null); A=\"\"; [ -n \"$pw\" ] && A=\"-u elastic:$pw\"; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec {{params.curl_pod_name}} -- curl -sk $A --max-time 5 \"http://{{params.http_service_name}}.elasticsearch.svc:9200/{{params.index_name}}/_count\" >/dev/null 2>&1; true'"
        timeout_sec: 20

oracle:
  verify:
    commands:
      - command: "python3 cases/elasticsearch/seed-hosts-repair/oracle/oracle.py"
        timeout_sec: 120

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json
import os
import re
import subprocess
import sys

NAMESPACE = os.environ.get("BENCH_NAMESPACE", "elasticsearch")
SERVICE = os.environ.get("BENCH_PARAM_HTTP_SERVICE_NAME", "es-http")
# ES 8.x runs with security enabled, so the HTTPS API requires authenticating as
# the elastic superuser. When this case inherits a secured cluster from an
# earlier workflow stage, read its password from the secret that stage created
# so the oracle's queries aren't rejected with 401. Absent secret -> None -> no
# -u, so a standalone unsecured cluster still works.
PASSWORD_SECRET = os.environ.get("BENCH_PARAM_ELASTIC_PASSWORD_SECRET_NAME", "elastic-password")
PASSWORD_KEY = os.environ.get("BENCH_PARAM_ELASTIC_PASSWORD_KEY", "password")
# Hint for the Elasticsearch pod app label. Used as an override when it matches a
# live StatefulSet's selector; otherwise the real selector label is detected
# live from the cluster. The env PERSISTS across stages, so a workflow's
# inherited ES cluster may label its pods differently than this case's standalone
# default of 'es-cluster'.
CLUSTER_PREFIX_HINT = os.environ.get("BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX", "es-cluster")
INDEX = os.environ.get("BENCH_PARAM_INDEX_NAME", "app-data")
DEFAULT_SCHEME = "http"
_SCHEME = None


def run(cmd):
    return subprocess.run(cmd, text=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE)


def _elastic_password():
    """Fetch the elastic-user password from its secret (base64-decoded), or None."""
    import base64
    r = run(["kubectl", "-n", NAMESPACE, "get", "secret", PASSWORD_SECRET,
             "-o", "jsonpath={.data." + PASSWORD_KEY + "}"])
    if r.returncode != 0 or not r.stdout.strip():
        return None
    try:
        return base64.b64decode(r.stdout.strip()).decode()
    except Exception:
        return None


ELASTIC_PASSWORD = None  # set in main() once kubectl is reachable


_APP_LABEL = None


def resolve_app_label():
    """Resolve the 'app=<value>' selector for the live ES pods.

    Priority: the BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX hint when some live StatefulSet
    actually selects on app=<hint> (explicit override wins) -> the app label of
    the namespace's Elasticsearch StatefulSet detected live -> the hint.
    Workflow-agnostic: adapts to an inherited cluster labelled e.g.
    app=elasticsearch instead of app=es-cluster.
    """
    global _APP_LABEL
    if _APP_LABEL is not None:
        return _APP_LABEL
    res = run(["kubectl", "-n", NAMESPACE, "get", "sts", "-o", "json"])
    labels = []
    if res.returncode == 0:
        try:
            for sts in json.loads(res.stdout).get("items", []):
                spec = sts.get("spec", {})
                containers = spec.get("template", {}).get("spec", {}).get("containers", [])
                if "elasticsearch" not in " ".join(c.get("image", "") for c in containers):
                    continue
                app = (spec.get("selector", {}).get("matchLabels", {}) or {}).get("app")
                ts = sts.get("metadata", {}).get("creationTimestamp", "")
                if app:
                    labels.append((app, ts))
        except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError):
            pass
    if any(app == CLUSTER_PREFIX_HINT for app, _ in labels):
        _APP_LABEL = f"app={CLUSTER_PREFIX_HINT}"
        return _APP_LABEL
    if labels:
        labels.sort(key=lambda x: (x[1] or ""))
        _APP_LABEL = f"app={labels[0][0]}"
        return _APP_LABEL
    _APP_LABEL = f"app={CLUSTER_PREFIX_HINT}"
    return _APP_LABEL


APP_LABEL = resolve_app_label()


def _list_es_statefulset_replicas():
    """Return {sts_name: spec.replicas} for the namespace's Elasticsearch
    StatefulSets (those whose pod template runs an Elasticsearch image)."""
    res = run(["kubectl", "-n", NAMESPACE, "get", "sts", "-o", "json"])
    if res.returncode != 0:
        return {}
    try:
        items = json.loads(res.stdout).get("items", [])
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError):
        return {}
    out = {}
    for sts in items:
        spec = sts.get("spec", {})
        containers = spec.get("template", {}).get("spec", {}).get("containers", [])
        if "elasticsearch" not in " ".join(c.get("image", "") for c in containers):
            continue
        name = sts.get("metadata", {}).get("name")
        replicas = spec.get("replicas")
        if name and isinstance(replicas, int):
            out[name] = replicas
    return out


def _sts_name_for_node(node_name):
    """Map a live ES node name to its backing StatefulSet name.

    An ES node name equals its pod name, which for a StatefulSet pod is
    ``<statefulset-name>-<ordinal>`` (e.g. ``es-cluster-0`` -> ``es-cluster``,
    ``es-data-1`` -> ``es-data``). Strip the trailing ``-<digits>`` ordinal.
    Returns the node name unchanged if it carries no ordinal suffix.
    """
    if not node_name:
        return node_name
    return re.sub(r"-\d+$", "", node_name)


def _live_sts_names(node_names):
    """Set of StatefulSet names actually backing the live cluster nodes.

    Derived by stripping each live node name's ordinal. This excludes
    accumulated/stale/other ES StatefulSets in the (persisted) namespace that
    back no node in the queried cluster.
    """
    return {_sts_name_for_node(n) for n in node_names if n}


def _resolve_expected_nodes(node_names, default=3):
    """Expected node count to enforce, derived from the LIVE cluster.

    The expected total is the DESIRED topology of ONLY the StatefulSets that
    actually back the live cluster (the nodes returned by ``_cat/nodes``): the
    sum of spec.replicas over just those StatefulSets. The namespace PERSISTS
    across workflow stages and accumulates multiple ES clusters, so summing
    replicas across *every* ES StatefulSet overcounts versus the single cluster
    the oracle queries. Restricting to the live cluster's StatefulSets fixes that.

    Using DESIRED spec.replicas (not the live count) keeps the check strict: a
    node that FAILED to join leaves its StatefulSet "live" (siblings are up) but
    absent from ``_cat/nodes``, so EXPECTED stays above the actual count -- no
    masking. The LIVE cluster is derived FIRST; the param is a FALLBACK used only
    when no live StatefulSets resolve (e.g. _cat/nodes failed or returned empty).
    """
    # Derive from the LIVE cluster FIRST (workflow-agnostic + composition-aware):
    # sum spec.replicas over the active ES StatefulSets -- the base cluster PLUS
    # any nodeset a PRIOR stage added to the same cluster (e.g. an inherited
    # transform nodeset). A static EXPECTED_NODES param encodes a standalone
    # baseline that wrongly ignores such inherited nodesets, so it is demoted to a
    # FALLBACK below, used only when the live cluster cannot be resolved.
    # Scope the sum to ONLY the ES StatefulSets that actually back a live node
    # returned by ``_cat/nodes`` (the queried cluster). The namespace PERSISTS and
    # ACCUMULATES several ES clusters/nodesets across stages; summing spec.replicas
    # over *every* ES StatefulSet overcounts the single cluster the oracle queries.
    # Mapping each live node name -> its StatefulSet (strip the pod ordinal) scopes
    # the sum. Using DESIRED spec.replicas stays strict: the repaired StatefulSet's
    # spec.replicas stays at its target (3) even while a node is missing, so a node
    # that failed to rejoin leaves the sum above the live _cat/nodes count.
    live_sts = _live_sts_names(node_names)
    if live_sts:
        res = run(["kubectl", "-n", NAMESPACE, "get", "sts", "-o", "json"])
        if res.returncode == 0:
            try:
                items = json.loads(res.stdout).get("items", [])
            except Exception:
                items = []
            desired = 0
            for sts in items:
                spec = sts.get("spec", {}) or {}
                name = (sts.get("metadata", {}) or {}).get("name")
                if name not in live_sts:
                    continue
                containers = spec.get("template", {}).get("spec", {}).get("containers", []) or []
                if "elasticsearch" not in " ".join(c.get("image", "") for c in containers):
                    continue
                replicas = spec.get("replicas")
                being_deleted = (sts.get("metadata", {}) or {}).get("deletionTimestamp") is not None
                if isinstance(replicas, int) and replicas > 0 and not being_deleted:
                    desired += replicas
            if desired > 0:
                return desired

    # Fallbacks (live cluster unresolvable): explicit param, then the default.
    for key in ("BENCH_PARAM_EXPECTED_NODE_COUNT", "BENCH_PARAM_EXPECTED_NODES"):
        val = os.environ.get(key)
        if val is not None and str(val).strip():
            try:
                return int(val)
            except ValueError:
                pass
    return default


def _probe_scheme(scheme):
    """True if the ES HTTP API answers on the given scheme (auth-agnostic)."""
    result = run([
        "kubectl", "-n", NAMESPACE, "exec", "curl-test", "--",
        "curl", "-s", "-S", "-k", "-o", "/dev/null",
        "-w", "%{http_code}", "--max-time", "5", f"{scheme}://{SERVICE}:9200/",
    ])
    code = (result.stdout or "").strip()
    return result.returncode == 0 and code.isdigit() and code != "000"


def detect_scheme():
    """Detect the cluster's live HTTP scheme (default first, then the other)."""
    global _SCHEME
    if _SCHEME is not None:
        return _SCHEME
    for scheme in (DEFAULT_SCHEME, "https" if DEFAULT_SCHEME == "http" else "http"):
        if _probe_scheme(scheme):
            _SCHEME = scheme
            return _SCHEME
    _SCHEME = DEFAULT_SCHEME
    return _SCHEME


def curl_json(path, errors):
    scheme = detect_scheme()
    # The client deadline must exceed any server-side ``wait_for`` in `path`,
    # otherwise curl aborts (exit 28) before ES can answer. The retry loop in
    # main() does the real waiting, so each call's server wait stays short (10s)
    # and --max-time (20) comfortably exceeds it.
    cmd = [
        "kubectl",
        "-n",
        NAMESPACE,
        "exec",
        "curl-test",
        "--",
        "curl",
        "-s",
        "-S",
        "-k",
    ]
    if ELASTIC_PASSWORD:
        cmd += ["-u", f"elastic:{ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"]
    cmd += [
        "--max-time",
        "20",
        f"{scheme}://{SERVICE}:9200{path}",
    ]
    result = run(cmd)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        detail = result.stderr.strip() or result.stdout.strip() or f"command terminated with exit code {result.returncode}"
        errors.append(f"Failed to query {path}: {detail}")
        return None
    output = result.stdout.strip()
    if not output:
        errors.append(f"Empty response for {path}")
        return None
    try:
        return json.loads(output)
    except json.JSONDecodeError:
        errors.append(f"Failed to parse JSON from {path}")
        return None


def evaluate():
    """Run one full snapshot of the seed-hosts checks; return the list of errors."""
    errors = []

    # Resolve the live cluster's node list FIRST: the expected node count is
    # derived from only the StatefulSets that actually back these live nodes
    # (the persisted namespace accumulates several ES clusters across stages, so
    # summing every ES StatefulSet's replicas overcounts the queried cluster).
    nodes = curl_json("/_cat/nodes?format=json", errors)
    node_names = []
    if isinstance(nodes, list):
        node_names = [n.get("name") for n in nodes if n.get("name")]
    expected_nodes = _resolve_expected_nodes(node_names, default=3)

    health = curl_json(
        f"/_cluster/health?wait_for_status=yellow&wait_for_nodes={expected_nodes}&timeout=10s",
        errors,
    )
    if isinstance(health, dict):
        status = health.get("status")
        if status not in {"yellow", "green"}:
            errors.append(f"Cluster health status expected yellow/green, got {status}")
        if health.get("number_of_nodes") != expected_nodes:
            errors.append(
                f"Expected {expected_nodes} nodes, got {health.get('number_of_nodes')}"
            )

    if isinstance(nodes, list):
        if len(node_names) != expected_nodes:
            errors.append(f"Expected {expected_nodes} nodes in _cat/nodes, got {len(node_names)}")

    count = curl_json(f"/{INDEX}/_count", errors)
    if isinstance(count, dict):
        if "count" not in count:
            errors.append("Unable to verify app-data count")

    return errors


def main():
    global ELASTIC_PASSWORD
    ELASTIC_PASSWORD = _elastic_password()

    # A multi-node ES cluster can flap at the edge of readiness under load: a
    # node briefly drops from the cluster during GC or shard recovery even though
    # it is stably green. A single snapshot can catch that transient and report a
    # false node-count miss. So verify the STABLE converged state: re-evaluate for
    # up to ~75s and pass on the first clean snapshot. This does not loosen the
    # N-node/green requirement -- a genuinely degraded cluster fails every attempt.
    import time
    deadline = time.monotonic() + 75
    errors = evaluate()
    while errors and time.monotonic() < deadline:
        time.sleep(8)
        errors = evaluate()

    if errors:
        print("Seed hosts repair verification failed:", file=sys.stderr)
        for err in errors:
            print(f"  - {err}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1

    print("Seed hosts repair verified")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

APP_LABEL = resolve_app_label()


def _list_es_statefulset_replicas():
    """Return {sts_name: spec.replicas} for the namespace's Elasticsearch
    StatefulSets (those whose pod template runs an Elasticsearch image)."""
    res = run(["kubectl", "-n", NAMESPACE, "get", "sts", "-o", "json"])
    if res.returncode != 0:
        return {}
    try:
        items = json.loads(res.stdout).get("items", [])
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError):
        return {}
    out = {}
    for sts in items:
        spec = sts.get("spec", {})
        containers = spec.get("template", {}).get("spec", {}).get("containers", [])
        if "elasticsearch" not in " ".join(c.get("image", "") for c in containers):
            continue
        name = sts.get("metadata", {}).get("name")
        replicas = spec.get("replicas")
        if name and isinstance(replicas, int):
            out[name] = replicas
    return out


def _sts_name_for_node(node_name):
    """Map a live ES node name to its backing StatefulSet name.

    An ES node name equals its pod name, which for a StatefulSet pod is
    ``<statefulset-name>-<ordinal>`` (e.g. ``es-cluster-0`` -> ``es-cluster``,
    ``es-data-1`` -> ``es-data``). Strip the trailing ``-<digits>`` ordinal.
    Returns the node name unchanged if it carries no ordinal suffix.
    """
    if not node_name:
        return node_name
    return re.sub(r"-\d+$", "", node_name)


def _live_sts_names(node_names):
    """Set of StatefulSet names actually backing the live cluster nodes.

    Derived by stripping each live node name's ordinal. This excludes
    accumulated/stale/other ES StatefulSets in the (persisted) namespace that
    back no node in the queried cluster.
    """
    return {_sts_name_for_node(n) for n in node_names if n}


def _resolve_expected_nodes(node_names, default=3):
    """Expected node count to enforce, derived from the LIVE cluster.

    The expected total is the DESIRED topology of ONLY the StatefulSets that
    actually back the live cluster (the nodes returned by ``_cat/nodes``): the
    sum of spec.replicas over just those StatefulSets. The namespace PERSISTS
    across workflow stages and accumulates multiple ES clusters, so summing
    replicas across *every* ES StatefulSet overcounts versus the single cluster
    the oracle queries. Restricting to the live cluster's StatefulSets fixes that.

    Using DESIRED spec.replicas (not the live count) keeps the check strict: a
    node that FAILED to join leaves its StatefulSet "live" (siblings are up) but
    absent from ``_cat/nodes``, so EXPECTED stays above the actual count -- no
    masking. The LIVE cluster is derived FIRST; the param is a FALLBACK used only
    when no live StatefulSets resolve (e.g. _cat/nodes failed or returned empty).
    """
    # Derive from the LIVE cluster FIRST (workflow-agnostic + composition-aware):
    # sum spec.replicas over the active ES StatefulSets -- the base cluster PLUS
    # any nodeset a PRIOR stage added to the same cluster (e.g. an inherited
    # transform nodeset). A static EXPECTED_NODES param encodes a standalone
    # baseline that wrongly ignores such inherited nodesets, so it is demoted to a
    # FALLBACK below, used only when the live cluster cannot be resolved.
    # Scope the sum to ONLY the ES StatefulSets that actually back a live node
    # returned by ``_cat/nodes`` (the queried cluster). The namespace PERSISTS and
    # ACCUMULATES several ES clusters/nodesets across stages; summing spec.replicas
    # over *every* ES StatefulSet overcounts the single cluster the oracle queries.
    # Mapping each live node name -> its StatefulSet (strip the pod ordinal) scopes
    # the sum. Using DESIRED spec.replicas stays strict: the repaired StatefulSet's
    # spec.replicas stays at its target (3) even while a node is missing, so a node
    # that failed to rejoin leaves the sum above the live _cat/nodes count.
    live_sts = _live_sts_names(node_names)
    if live_sts:
        res = run(["kubectl", "-n", NAMESPACE, "get", "sts", "-o", "json"])
        if res.returncode == 0:
            try:
                items = json.loads(res.stdout).get("items", [])
            except Exception:
                items = []
            desired = 0
            for sts in items:
                spec = sts.get("spec", {}) or {}
                name = (sts.get("metadata", {}) or {}).get("name")
                if name not in live_sts:
                    continue
                containers = spec.get("template", {}).get("spec", {}).get("containers", []) or []
                if "elasticsearch" not in " ".join(c.get("image", "") for c in containers):
                    continue
                replicas = spec.get("replicas")
                being_deleted = (sts.get("metadata", {}) or {}).get("deletionTimestamp") is not None
                if isinstance(replicas, int) and replicas > 0 and not being_deleted:
                    desired += replicas
            if desired > 0:
                return desired

    # Fallbacks (live cluster unresolvable): explicit param, then the default.
    for key in ("BENCH_PARAM_EXPECTED_NODE_COUNT", "BENCH_PARAM_EXPECTED_NODES"):
        val = os.environ.get(key)
        if val is not None and str(val).strip():
            try:
                return int(val)
            except ValueError:
                pass
    return default


def _probe_scheme(scheme):
    """True if the ES HTTP API answers on the given scheme (auth-agnostic)."""
    result = run([
        "kubectl", "-n", NAMESPACE, "exec", "curl-test", "--",
        "curl", "-s", "-S", "-k", "-o", "/dev/null",
        "-w", "%{http_code}", "--max-time", "5", f"{scheme}://{SERVICE}:9200/",
    ])
    code = (result.stdout or "").strip()
    return result.returncode == 0 and code.isdigit() and code != "000"


def detect_scheme():
    """Detect the cluster's live HTTP scheme (default first, then the other)."""
    global _SCHEME
    if _SCHEME is not None:
        return _SCHEME
    for scheme in (DEFAULT_SCHEME, "https" if DEFAULT_SCHEME == "http" else "http"):
        if _probe_scheme(scheme):
            _SCHEME = scheme
            return _SCHEME
    _SCHEME = DEFAULT_SCHEME
    return _SCHEME


def curl_json(path, errors):
    scheme = detect_scheme()
    # The client deadline must exceed any server-side ``wait_for`` in `path`,
    # otherwise curl aborts (exit 28) before ES can answer. The retry loop in
    # main() does the real waiting, so each call's server wait stays short (10s)
    # and --max-time (20) comfortably exceeds it.
    cmd = [
        "kubectl",
        "-n",
        NAMESPACE,
        "exec",
        "curl-test",
        "--",
        "curl",
        "-s",
        "-S",
        "-k",
    ]
    if ELASTIC_PASSWORD:
        cmd += ["-u", f"elastic:{ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"]
    cmd += [
        "--max-time",
        "20",
        f"{scheme}://{SERVICE}:9200{path}",
    ]
    result = run(cmd)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        detail = result.stderr.strip() or result.stdout.strip() or f"command terminated with exit code {result.returncode}"
        errors.append(f"Failed to query {path}: {detail}")
        return None
    output = result.stdout.strip()
    if not output:
        errors.append(f"Empty response for {path}")
        return None
    try:
        return json.loads(output)
    except json.JSONDecodeError:
        errors.append(f"Failed to parse JSON from {path}")
        return None


def evaluate():
    """Run one full snapshot of the seed-hosts checks; return the list of errors."""
    errors = []

    # Resolve the live cluster's node list FIRST: the expected node count is
    # derived from only the StatefulSets that actually back these live nodes
    # (the persisted namespace accumulates several ES clusters across stages, so
    # summing every ES StatefulSet's replicas overcounts the queried cluster).
    nodes = curl_json("/_cat/nodes?format=json", errors)
    node_names = []
    if isinstance(nodes, list):
        node_names = [n.get("name") for n in nodes if n.get("name")]
    expected_nodes = _resolve_expected_nodes(node_names, default=3)

    health = curl_json(
        f"/_cluster/health?wait_for_status=yellow&wait_for_nodes={expected_nodes}&timeout=10s",
        errors,
    )
    if isinstance(health, dict):
        status = health.get("status")
        if status not in {"yellow", "green"}:
            errors.append(f"Cluster health status expected yellow/green, got {status}")
        if health.get("number_of_nodes") != expected_nodes:
            errors.append(
                f"Expected {expected_nodes} nodes, got {health.get('number_of_nodes')}"
            )

    if isinstance(nodes, list):
        if len(node_names) != expected_nodes:
            errors.append(f"Expected {expected_nodes} nodes in _cat/nodes, got {len(node_names)}")

    count = curl_json(f"/{INDEX}/_count", errors)
    if isinstance(count, dict):
        if "count" not in count:
            errors.append("Unable to verify app-data count")

    return errors


def main():
    global ELASTIC_PASSWORD
    ELASTIC_PASSWORD = _elastic_password()

    # A multi-node ES cluster can flap at the edge of readiness under load: a
    # node briefly drops from the cluster during GC or shard recovery even though
    # it is stably green. A single snapshot can catch that transient and report a
    # false node-count miss. So verify the STABLE converged state: re-evaluate for
    # up to ~75s and pass on the first clean snapshot. This does not loosen the
    # N-node/green requirement -- a genuinely degraded cluster fails every attempt.
    import time
    deadline = time.monotonic() + 75
    errors = evaluate()
    while errors and time.monotonic() < deadline:
        time.sleep(8)
        errors = evaluate()

    if errors:
        print("Seed hosts repair verification failed:", file=sys.stderr)
        for err in errors:
            print(f"  - {err}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1

    print("Seed hosts repair verified")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

content.thinking:
Now I understand the KARMA semantics:

```python
# probe **passes** -> state already present, apply is skipped.
# probe **fails** + ``on_probe_fail="skip"`` (the default, used by setup units) 
#   -> the "not yet present" signal is expected, so apply runs to establish it.
```

So for `on_probe_fail: skip` (default):
- probe PASSES → already present → skip apply
- probe FAILS → not yet present → run apply
- probe PASSES → already present → skip apply
- probe FAILS → retry probe (it's a gate that MUST pass)

For the `es_env_ready` unit, the probe checks whether the Elasticsearch cluster, its service, and at least one pod are running. If they're already there, apply gets skipped since the cluster is ready to use. If they're missing, apply runs to build a fresh cluster and inject the fault by setting an incorrect discovery seed hosts configuration.

For the `seed_hosts_index_fixture` unit, the probe checks if the app-data index exists via HTTP. Since the cluster uses TLS/HTTPS, the HTTP request fails and curl exits with an error code, which causes the entire probe command to fail even though grep never sees any output.

When the probe fails, it triggers the apply step, which tries to seed the index using HTTP curl commands. These requests would fail silently because HTTPS is required, but the apply always succeeds due to the `true` at the end.

Looking at the current state, all 5 ES pods are running (es-cluster-0, es-cluster-1, es-cluster-2, es-data-0, es-data-1), the cluster is GREEN with 5 nodes, and the app-data index exists and is readable. I'm wondering where the es-data-0 and es-data-1 pods came from since they seem like a separate nodeset, and I'm checking the `es_env_ready` probe which looks for `app=es-cluster` to understand what's being validated.

The precondition didn't inject the fault this run since the probe passed and apply was skipped. The `seed_hosts_index_fixture` ran its apply, which attempted HTTP but failed, though the app-data index already existed. This suggests the "failed to rejoin" scenario was either set up by a previous stage or the cluster's current state is a partially fixed broken state from earlier. The es-cluster pods restarted about 9 minutes ago despite the StatefulSet being up for 70 minutes, which is suspicious—especially since es-cluster-2 had its PVC deleted and might be trying to rejoin as a fresh node. For `es_env_ready`, only the probe ran and passed, so the apply was skipped—meaning the cluster was already running and this stage didn't inject any fault. For `seed_hosts_index_fixture`, the probe failed due to the TLS endpoint returning an empty reply, triggering the apply and verify steps.

The fault must have come from a previous stage that corrupted the ConfigMap with wrong DNS names and deleted/recreated es-cluster-2. But I'm seeing all 5 nodes healthy and the cluster in GREEN state, which contradicts the expected fault where es-cluster-2 should be unable to rejoin due to the misconfigured discovery.seed_hosts.

Looking at the es-cluster-0 logs, I can see the master node changed and nodes were added to the cluster, including es-cluster-2. This suggests es-cluster-2 somehow managed to rejoin despite the corrupted ConfigMap—possibly because it retained cached cluster state from before the fault was injected, allowing it to bypass the broken seed_hosts configuration.

Looking at the es-cluster-0 logs, it rejoined at 21:15:33. The stage_09 precondition ran its probes in sequence: `es_env_ready` passed since pods were there, but `seed_hosts_index_fixture` failed because it was trying HTTP against an HTTPS-only cluster, returning exit code 52. The apply then attempted to run but silently failed due to the same protocol mismatch.

Now I see the actual semantics from the KARMA code—`on_probe_fail: skip` is the default behavior that means "run the apply when the probe fails." The naming confused me: "skip" refers to skipping the apply when the probe *passes*, not when it fails. So the logic is: probe passes means the state already exists (skip apply), but probe fails with `on_probe_fail: skip` means run the apply to establish that state. With `on_probe_fail: error`, a failed probe would instead trigger an error and retry as a gate.

For `es_env_ready`, the probe checks if the Elasticsearch cluster is up (checking for the StatefulSet, service, and running pods), and if it passes, the apply is skipped since the cluster already exists. If it fails, the apply runs to build the cluster from scratch and inject the fault. But for `seed_hosts_index_fixture`, there's a mismatch: the probe tries to check if the app-data index exists via HTTP, but the cluster uses HTTPS, so the probe fails and triggers the apply. However, the apply also uses HTTP, which would also fail—except both the apply and verify steps end with `true`, so they always exit successfully regardless of whether they actually accomplished anything.

So the `seed_hosts_index_fixture` precondition appears to succeed even though the HTTP requests fail against the HTTPS cluster. Yet I can confirm the app-data index does exist with one document containing `{"msg": "seed"}`. This document must have come from either a previous fresh build's `es_env_ready` apply or from the `seed_hosts_index_fixture` apply itself, which also seeds that exact document. Looking back at the `es_env_ready` apply steps, it deletes and recreates the namespace, applies resources, waits for pods, runs health checks via HTTP (which would fail), seeds the index via HTTP (also fails), corrupts a ConfigMap, and scales resources.

The health check uses an HTTP curl against the Elasticsearch service with a 5-second timeout, looping 30 times with 2-second intervals before exiting with status 1. If the apply fails, KARMA should mark it as failed unless there's retry logic involved. But since the current cluster is HTTPS-secured, the `es_env_ready` probe must have actually passed, meaning the apply was skipped entirely—no rebuild, no fault injection. So the situation is that all previous stages built up an HTTPS-secured Elasticsearch cluster with 5 nodes.

The seed-hosts-repair scenario precondition ran: the `es_env_ready` probe passed (cluster running) so the apply was skipped, while the `seed_hosts_index_fixture` probe failed due to HTTP vs HTTPS mismatch, causing the apply to run but fail silently. No fault was actually injected, and the cluster is running fine with all 5 nodes healthy. This means the seed-hosts-repair scenario isn't actually set up in this run. The oracle will verify cluster health is yellow or green, all 5 nodes appear in the nodes endpoint, and app-data remains readable.

Then when stage_09 started, the `es_env_ready` probe saw healthy running pods and skipped the apply, so the fault injection never happened.
